summary:
Metastatic carcinoma to cervical nodes with an unknown primary is classified as distant metastatic disease, which qualifies as an absolute contraindication to neck dissection according to established guidelines. Neck dissection is inappropriate in these circumstances due to systemic spread, rendering surgery futile rather than beneficial.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only two sentences focused solely on core clinical principles.)
